notes.author: Dr. David Park, PGY-1 / Dr. Maria Castro, PGY-3 / Dr. Sarah Beckman, attending
notes.preview: Admission H&P for 23F with active SLE + new inflammatory myositis + severe oropharyngeal dysphagia presenting with multi-system deterioration: BMI 15.7 (30.3% weight loss), anasarca, 'flag sign' hair, kwashiorkor-pattern scaling dermatosis, proximal weakness 2-3/5, dysphonia, oral ulcers. Labs: Hgb 7.8, WBC 2.30, albumin 1.6, prealbumin 6, CK 2360, anti-dsDNA 48, C3 58, C4 5, electrolyte derangements with refeeding risk. Working unifying dx: SLE -> myositis -> dysphagia -> starvation -> kwashiorkor.

notes.author: Dr. Sarah Beckman / Dr. Maria Castro
notes.preview: 4-day admission for myositis/dysphagia workup ending in AMA discharge. MBSS 2/9 showed mild oropharyngeal dysphagia with laryngeal penetration of thin liquids; nectar-thick/pureed diet with chin-tuck. Skin biopsy taken 2/10 (pending). Multidisciplinary consults: rheum, neuro, GI, ID, derm, nutrition. EMG and muscle biopsy scheduled but NOT done due to AMA discharge. Strong outpatient bridge planned; patient agreed to keep follow-ups.

notes.author: Dr. James Tran
notes.preview: 23F with active SLE on HCQ/MMF/prednisone presenting with new oropharyngeal dysphagia + proximal weakness + voice change + 18.9% weight loss (now 41.2 kg). CK 1840, aldolase 22, anti-dsDNA 38, low complements, mild proteinuria, oral ulcer. Diagnosis: SLE flare with new inflammatory myositis. Admitted (became NOTE011).

notes.author: Dr. Andre Williams
notes.preview: 6-week PCP follow-up. SLE responding well to MMF + HCQ + prednisone taper. Joint pain much better, rash mostly resolved, weight up to 47.2 kg, mood improved (PHQ-9 7). NEW SYMPTOM: occasional bolus dysphagia with dry foods x 2-3 weeks. Bedside swallow screen equivocal. Ordered CK / aldolase / LDH to screen for occult myositis.

notes.author: Dr. Jessica Tanaka
notes.preview: 5-day post-ED urgent re-evaluation. Modest clinical improvement on prednisone 30 mg + restarted HCQ. Added mycophenolate 500 mg BID (planning to escalate to 1 g BID). Pre-immunosuppression infection screen ordered. Contraception counseling with mother. Strong adherence and access plan with social work.

notes.author: Dr. Priya Shah
notes.preview: 23-year-old woman with UCTD/probable early SLE and Hashimoto's thyroiditis presenting with worsening fatigue, joint pain, weight loss, intermittent rash after running out of HCQ for 3 weeks. Weight 43.8 kg (down 7 kg / 13.8% from baseline). Anti-dsDNA now positive, complements low, mild proteinuria, tri-lineage cytopenia. Recommended admission; patient declined. Strong outpatient plan and return precautions.

notes.author: Dr. Andre Williams
notes.preview: Just-turned-23-year-old woman with UCTD off HCQ for 5 weeks (PAP denied). Joint pain markedly worse, new photosensitive rash on V-area chest, 4.4 kg weight loss (~8.7%), new mild cytopenia (Hgb 11.1, WBC 3.85), low albumin (3.4), elevated ESR/CRP, PHQ-9 11. Bridged HCQ via low-cost cash pay; restarted with social work re-engagement and urgent rheum re-evaluation requested.

notes.author: Walgreens Patterson Park / Esperanza Health staff
notes.preview: HCQ refill could not be filled — manufacturer PAP renewal denied. Patient out of HCQ since ~8/12/2026. Reports worsening joint pain, fatigue, possible new rash, hair shedding, loose pants. Urgent care coordination and PCP follow-up arranged.

notes.author: Diana Mercado, LCSW
notes.preview: Medicaid application DENIED 5/29/2026 due to incomplete income documentation; appeal filed today. PAP applications for HCQ submitted. Food insecurity worsening — family stretching food, patient skipping breakfast. Mental health concerns emerging. Comprehensive social support plan activated.

notes.author: Dr. Jessica Tanaka
notes.preview: First specialist evaluation 3 months after symptom onset. Assessment: undifferentiated CTD, possible early SLE; co-existing Hashimoto's. Continue HCQ, add low-dose prednisone 10 mg taper x ~7 weeks for symptomatic control. Hand X-rays show non-erosive synovitis. Social work re-engagement. Follow-up in 3 months pending access.

notes.author: Dr. Manuel Reyes
notes.preview: 2-week follow-up. Tolerating HCQ and levothyroxine. Persistent symmetric synovitis. Send-out labs from 1/12 returned: anti-Sm, anti-dsDNA, anti-Ro/La, anti-CCP all negative; hep B/C/HIV negative; parvovirus IgM negative (IgG positive — past); TPO antibody positive (Hashimoto's confirmed); UA bland. Continue current treatment pending rheum.

notes.author: Dr. Manuel Reyes
notes.preview: 22-year-old Spanish-speaking woman presenting with 6-month history of progressive joint pain, fatigue, and hair loss. Interpreter used. Symmetric polyarthritis of hands, wrists, ankles. ANA positive 1:80. Started empirically on hydroxychloroquine and levothyroxine pending rheumatology referral.
